nativeRange.habitat: Still or slow-moving warm water: coastal streams, ponds, swamps, canals, tidal ditches, and brackish estuaries; highly vegetated areas near the surface

decorPreferences: dense planting (Java fern, hornwort, water sprite, floating plants)
open swimming areas for male dorsal display
driftwood or rocks as territory markers

diet.notes: Predominantly plant matter — algae wafers, spirulina flakes, blanched vegetables (zucchini, spinach), and vegetable-based flakes are essential. Also accepts high-quality flake, pellets, and frozen foods (brine shrimp, daphnia). Wild fish feed primarily on algae, rotifers, and small crustaceans. Soft aquatic plants may be nibbled.

avoidWith: cherry shrimp and small dwarf shrimp (fry predation risk)
small fish that may be harassed by mature males

summary: The largest Molly species, males develop a spectacular tall sail-like dorsal fin. Generally advised to be kept in hard, alkaline water; euryhaline and broadly brackish-tolerant. Intermediate care due to size and water quality demands.
careNotes: The Sailfin Molly is widely regarded as the largest member of the Molly group, with mature males reaching up to 15 cm and developing a dramatically extended dorsal fin that is displayed during courtship. A tank of at least 150 L with a minimum 120 cm footprint has been recommended to give males adequate space to display and to house a typical trio (1 male, 2+ females). Maintain hard, alkaline water (gH 15–30, kH 8–20, pH 7.5–8.5); soft or acidic water has been associated with increased disease susceptibility and shortened lifespan. Mineral supplementation is advisable in soft-water areas. A 2:1 or 3:1 female-to-male ratio tends to reduce male harassment of females. The Sailfin Molly is euryhaline — it naturally inhabits coastal brackish and even marine waters, and many experienced keepers report improved health and reduced disease rates when a small amount of marine salt (e.g., SG 1.002–1.005) is added; this is not strictly required but is widely considered beneficial for long-term keeping. The trade name 'Marble Molly' refers to the same species (P. latipinna) in a mottled color morph. Sailfin Mollies have been crossed extensively with P. sphenops (Black Molly) to produce hybrid commercial strains; pure P. latipinna males are distinguishable by the large dorsal sail. See also: [molly] (P. sphenops) — the smaller common Molly; [balloon-molly] — a selectively-bred compressed-body morph derived from P. sphenops.
breedingNotes: Prolific livebearers with gestation of approximately 6–8 weeks, producing up to 50 fry per brood. Females can store sperm and produce multiple broods from a single mating. No parental care is provided; dense planting substantially improves fry survival. Fry are born live and large enough to accept finely crushed flake and micro-pellets from birth. A 2–3:1 female-to-male ratio is generally recommended to reduce male harassment. Males begin exhibiting the distinctive dorsal sail as they mature.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish — Poecilia latipinna
primary.notes: Primary source. Provided adult size (males 12.5 cm, females 11 cm), temperature 21–26°C, pH 7.0–8.5, hardness 15–35 dH, native range, biotope, diet, and breeding notes. Aquarium Co-Op (https://www.aquariumcoop.com/blogs/aquarium/molly-fish-care) used as primary trade source — no dedicated P. latipinna page exists; general molly guide supplemented sailfin-specific data (tank size 29–55 gal for larger species, temperature 24–27°C, salt tolerance noted).

additional.name: FishBase — Poecilia latipinna
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (males to 15 cm TL, females to 10 cm), temperature (20–28°C), native range (Cape Fear NC to Veracruz MX), habitat (still/slow warm coastal waters), taxonomy (Poeciliidae, Cyprinodontiformes), diet (algae, rotifers, small crustaceans). Size spread: SeriouslyFish max male 12.5 cm vs FishBase 15 cm — used 15 cm per user brief and FishBase morphological maximum.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Molly Fish Care
additional.notes: General molly care guide; supplemented sailfin-specific size/tank guidance. No dedicated P. latipinna page available. Temperature 24–27°C and tank size 29–55 gal (110–208 L) for larger molly species cited; 150 L chosen as conservative round value within that range per playbook guidance.

fish.breedingNotes: Highly prolific livebearer; gestation ~6–8 weeks, up to 50 fry per brood. No specialised setup required beyond 2–3:1 female-to-male ratio and dense planting for fry cover. Fry accept micro-crushed flake from birth.
